description_long: This is a compact submachine gun with a minimalist, utilitarian design, reminiscent of the British Sten gun. The rifle features a straight, cylindrical barrel and a simple tubular receiver, both finished in a dark, matte metal. Its most distinctive feature is the skeletal wireframe stock, which is fixed and extends directly from the rear of the receiver, providing lightweight support. The magazine is a vertical, detachable box type, inserted beneath the receiver, and the trigger guard is integrated into the lower assembly. The rifle is equipped with basic iron sights, with no visible optics or advanced sighting systems. The overall construction is robust and functional, with exposed metal surfaces and minimal embellishments, suitable for urban environments where reliability and ease of use are prioritized.
description: A compact, utilitarian submachine gun with a skeletal wireframe stock, detachable box magazine, and matte metal finish. Its design is simple and robust, featuring basic iron sights and minimal external features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, skeletal stock, and vertical magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel end, narrow receiver profile, and minimal sight.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the skeletal stock and magazine from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the wireframe stock alignment and the back of the receiver.
